message:
perf(storage): short-circuit append paths when no messages were inserted

Adds five symmetric early-return guards plus a regression test so that
an idempotent re-merge of an already-stored conversation pays only the
read-side cost (the bounded existing-message lookup + the append-only
tail probe added in dcbd5dc0) and skips every write-side substage.

Why this matters: cass's primary workload is the indexer watching live
agent sessions, where the same conversation is repeatedly re-imported
as new messages arrive. Once the watch has caught up to the latest
message, every subsequent re-import resolves to "the incoming message
list is already fully present in the DB" — i.e. inserted_indices comes
back empty. Before this commit those no-op merges still:
  * committed the surrounding transaction;
  * called flush_pending_fts_entries (a no-op flush, but still the
    function-call + scratch-buffer rotation);
  * issued an UPDATE conversations SET ended_at = MAX(...) WHERE id = ?
    that always re-wrote the same ended_at value;
  * ran the analytics + daily_stats rollup substages with empty input.

After this commit, when inserted_indices is empty we short-circuit out
before any of those steps run, so the no-op path is purely read-bound.

Concretely:

  * franken_append_messages_in_tx wrapper: return outcome immediately
    when inserted_indices is empty so the caller does not commit a
    no-op transaction.
  * Inner append-in-tx body: identical guard before flush_pending_fts_entries.
  * append_existing_conversation_with_profile: the same guard, plus
    bumping `profile.invocations`, `profile.messages`, and
    `profile.total_duration` so the profile still reflects "we were
    asked to merge N messages and finished in T ns" — just with all
    the *_duration substage counters left at Duration::ZERO. Tests
    can therefore assert "no work happened in stage X" by reading
    those counters directly.
  * append_existing_conversation: same guard before its non-profiled
    flush path.
  * Two ended_at-update sites (the profiled and recovered merge paths)
    gain a leading `!inserted_indices.is_empty() &&` guard on the
    if-let-chain, so a no-op merge does not re-write the conversation
    row at all. This both eliminates the UPDATE round-trip and avoids
    spurious "row updated at T" telemetry for an idempotent merge.

Test:
  append_existing_conversation_noop_skips_append_tail_write_work
  - Inserts a 5-message base conversation, then re-inserts an
    "expanded" 10-message version (which becomes the canonical state).
  - Calls append_existing_conversation_with_profile a second time
    against the now-canonical 10-message conversation and asserts:
      * outcome.inserted_indices is empty (idempotent re-merge);
      * profile.invocations == 1 (the call did happen);
      * profile.messages == expanded.messages.len();
      * profile.inserted_messages == 0;
      * profile.conversation_row_duration == Duration::ZERO;
      * profile.analytics_duration == Duration::ZERO;
      * profile.commit_duration == Duration::ZERO.
  - The assertion set is the precise telemetry contract: profile is
    populated for the call but every write-side substage cost is zero.

This pairs cleanly with dcbd5dc0's append-only-tail fast path: that
commit made the read-side cheap by bounding the lookup; this commit
makes the write side zero when the read returns "nothing new".
